summary: Linked the fighter source file src/melee/ft/ft_0881.c by correcting two calls inside ft_800881D8 from ft_800887CC(fp) to ft_80088770(fp), then changing configure.py so melee/ft/ft_0881.c is built as Matching instead of NonMatching. The PR body had no explanation and there was no human review discussion, but the bot report showed linked code increasing by 4348 bytes.

key_files.why: Source file made matchable; two helper calls in ft_800881D8 were changed from ft_800887CC to ft_80088770.
Build object status for melee/ft/ft_0881.c was promoted from NonMatching to Matching.

what_changed: In ft_800881D8(Fighter* fp, int sfx_id, u8 sfx_vol, u8 sfx_pan), two branches now call ft_80088770(fp) instead of ft_800887CC(fp).
The two call-site changes occur after paths that conditionally call func_8038C5F8(..., fp->x221F_b4) and return; when that early-return condition does not apply, the fallback helper is ft_80088770.
No headers, structs, or symbol names were changed in the provided diff.
smart_moves: The fix was minimal: it changed only the callee identity at the mismatching call sites rather than restructuring control flow.
The same helper-call correction was applied consistently in both duplicated/symmetric branches of ft_800881D8.
The object was only promoted to Matching in configure.py after the source-level call corrections, keeping build status aligned with actual matching state.
decomp_lessons: When a function is structurally close but still nonmatching, verify that each call target is the exact expected helper; two similarly named address-based functions, ft_800887CC and ft_80088770, were not interchangeable here.
Repeated branch bodies should be audited in pairs: if one branch requires a callee correction, a parallel branch may need the same correction.

For decomp progress PRs, the final configure.py Matching/NonMatching flip is a useful signal that the source file now links, but the important matching tactic may be a very small semantic correction in the C source.
naming_conventions: The PR preserved existing address-style function names such as ft_800881D8, ft_800887CC, and ft_80088770.
The fighter pointer parameter name fp and field name x221F_b4 were kept unchanged.
The build configuration uses Object(Matching, "...") and Object(NonMatching, "...") status markers under MatchingFor(*versions).
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use exact callee selection to repair codegen/linking when two candidate helpers have similar names and signatures.
Promote a file from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py only after the source produces matching linked code.
Small call-target substitutions can unlock an entire object file; this PR linked ft_0881.c with only two source-line changes plus the configuration flip.

review_feedback.feedback: Report for GALE01 showed linked code at 27.89%, an increase of +0.11% and +4348 bytes; the report text also said "No changes" beyond the linked-code metric.
No human review comments or PR body rationale were present in the provided slice.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or document ft_80088770 or ft_800887CC.
Search for other ft_0881.c matching fixes involving ft_800881D8 or sound effect helper calls.
Look for adjacent fighter files promoted from NonMatching to Matching near ft_0877.c and ft_0892.c.
